data.content:
Now I have a full picture. Here are the **legacy patterns** found:

| # | Legacy Pattern | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `ToolsVersion` + XML namespace on `<Project>` | Use `<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">` |
| 2 | Explicit `<Import>` of Common.props & CSharp.targets | Removed — SDK handles this |
| 3 | Boilerplate: `Configuration`, `Platform`, `ProjectGuid`, `FileAlignment`, `Deterministic`, `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects` | All SDK defaults |
| 4 | Debug/Release `PropertyGroup` blocks | SDK provides sensible defaults |
| 5 | Explicit `<Compile Include="...">` items | SDK implicit globbing |
| 6 | Framework assembly `<Reference>` items (`System`, `System.Core`, etc.) | Implied by the SDK |
| 7 | `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` | SDK auto-generates; use project properties |
| 8 | `TargetFrameworkVersion v4.7.2` | `<TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>` |
| 9 | `RootNamespace` / `AssemblyName` matching project name | SDK defaults — can be omitted |

Let me apply all of these now.

arguments.old_str:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<Project ToolsVersion="15.0" xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/developer/msbuild/2003">
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props" Condition="Exists('$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props')" />
  <PropertyGroup>
    <Configuration Condition=" '$(Configuration)' == '' ">Debug</Configuration>
    <Platform Condition=" '$(Platform)' == '' ">AnyCPU</Platform>
    <ProjectGuid>{12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012}</ProjectGuid>
    <OutputType>Library</OutputType>
    <RootNamespace>LegacyApp</RootNamespace>
    <AssemblyName>LegacyApp</AssemblyName>
    <TargetFrameworkVersion>v4.7.2</TargetFrameworkVersion>
    <FileAlignment>512</FileAlignment>
    <AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>true</AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>
    <Deterministic>true</Deterministic>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Debug|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugSymbols>true</DebugSymbols>
    <DebugType>full</DebugType>
    <Optimize>false</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Debug\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>DEBUG;TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Release|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugType>pdbonly</DebugType>
    <Optimize>true</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Release\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Reference Include="System" />
    <Reference Include="System.Core" />
    <Reference Include="System.Data" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml.Linq" />
    <Reference Include="Microsoft.CSharp" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Include="Calculator.cs" />
    <Compile Include="StringHelper.cs" />
    <Compile Include="Properties\AssemblyInfo.cs" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildToolsPath)\Microsoft.CSharp.targets" />
</Project>
arguments.new_str:
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>
    <Description>A legacy-style project for modernization testing</Description>
    <Company>TestCo</Company>
    <Product>LegacyApp</Product>
    <Copyright>Copyright © 2024</Copyright>
    <Version>1.0.0.0</Version>
  </PropertyGroup>

</Project>

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a complete picture of the legacy project. Let me invoke the msbuild-modernization skill for guidance, then modernize the project.

Let me identify all legacy patterns:

1. **`ToolsVersion` attribute & XML namespace** on `<Project>` — SDK-style uses `<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">`
2. **Explicit `<Import>` of `Microsoft.Common.props` and `Microsoft.CSharp.targets`** — SDK handles these automatically
3. **Boilerplate PropertyGroups**: `Configuration`, `Platform`, `ProjectGuid`, `FileAlignment`, `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects`, `Deterministic` — all SDK defaults


4. **Debug/Release configuration blocks** — SDK provides sensible defaults
5. **Explicit `<Compile Include="...">`** items — SDK-style uses implicit globbing
6. **Framework assembly references** (`System`, `System.Core`, etc.) — implied by the SDK
7. **`Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs`** — SDK auto-generates assembly attributes; use project properties instead
8. **`TargetFrameworkVersion` v4.7.2** — becomes `<TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>`
9. **`OutputType` Library** — this is the default for SDK-style and can be omitted

Now I'll modernize the project file by removing AssemblyInfo.cs and moving those assembly attributes into the project properties instead. I'm creating a clean SDK-style project file with the essential metadata like target framework, namespace, assembly name, and version information all defined in the PropertyGroup.

Actually, since RootNamespace and AssemblyName default to the project file name, I can strip those out too and keep the file even leaner.

data.content:
Build succeeds with zero warnings. Here's the summary of what changed:

**47 → 12 lines** — the project went from verbose legacy XML to a clean SDK-style file.

### Changes made:
1. **`<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">`** — replaces `ToolsVersion`, XML namespace, and both `<Import>` statements
2. **`<TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>`** — replaces `TargetFrameworkVersion v4.7.2`
3. **Removed** all boilerplate (`Configuration`, `Platform`, `ProjectGuid`, `FileAlignment`, `Deterministic`, Debug/Release property groups) — SDK defaults
4. **Removed** explicit `<Compile>` items — SDK implicit globbing picks up `*.cs` automatically
5. **Removed** framework `<Reference>` items (`System`, `System.Core`, etc.) — implied by SDK
6. **Deleted `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs`** — assembly metadata is now expressed as project properties (`Description`, `Company`, `Product`, `Copyright`, `Version`)
